prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on P | Statement: EVA laminates with gel content over 92% are more likely to yellow and show less stable adhesion during aging.
2. [GOLD] Source: Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on P | Statement: EVA manufacturer STR recommends a minimum 53 N/cm peel strength after lamination by 180-degree peel to ensure 20-year module lifetime.
3. [SILVER] Source: Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on P | Statement: Tracy et al. proposed initial delamination avoidance thresholds of 160 J/m2 for encapsulant interfaces and 10 J/m2 for backsheet interfaces.
4. [SILVER] Source: Dependence of adhesion on degradation mechanisms of ethylene | Statement: Three degradation mechanisms dominate EVA adhesion loss sequentially: deacetylation initially, then beta-scission, followed by hydrolytic depolymeriza
5. [SILVER] Source: Dependence of adhesion on degradation mechanisms of ethylene | Statement: Glass/EVA interface experiences more degradation than EVA/cell, explaining why this initially stronger interface becomes preferential delamination sit
